title: Model resolver output (cycle-099 FR-3.9 6-stage resolver)
description: Per-resolution output of the FR-3.9 6-stage resolver per SDD §1.5.1. Single (skill, role) query produces one ResolutionResult. Success carries resolved_provider + resolved_model_id + resolution_path; failure carries error. Output is discriminated by presence of `error` (if present, failure; else success). The shape mirrors `tests/fixtures/model-resolution/*.yaml::expected.resolutions[]` so fixtures double as schema validation cases. Field ordering, key casing, and null-vs-absent semantics are pinned. Cross-runtime (Python canonical, bash + TS test runners) MUST emit byte-equal canonical JSON for the same (config, skill, role) input.

oneOf.comment: Resolution-level result: requires skill+role; carries either resolved_* fields or `error`. This is the primary output shape.

Fixture-level error (YAML parse failure or missing expected.resolutions block) — emitted by the golden runners when no (skill, role) tuple can be derived from the fixture. Has `fixture` + `error` only.

fixture.description: Optional context tag added by golden test runners (e.g., '01-happy-path-tier-tag'). The production resolver does NOT emit this field; only `tests/{python,bash,typescript}/golden_resolution.*` runners include it for cross-runtime traceability and sort-order verification. additionalProperties:false would reject this in production output, which is correct — production callers use the resolver's pure-function API and never set fixture.

resolved_model_id.description: Canonical model_id (e.g., 'claude-opus-4-7'). Required on success. Pattern matches the model_aliases_extra.id pattern from cycle-099 Sprint 2A.

resolution_path.description: Sequence of FR-3.9 stage outcomes that produced this resolution. Stage numbers ascending. Required on success.

error.description: Present iff resolution failed. Mutually exclusive with resolved_provider/resolved_model_id/resolution_path.

stage.description: FR-3.9 stage number. 0 reserved for pre-resolution validation (e.g., schema-load-time conflict detection per IMP-004). 1-6 are the six resolution stages (S1 explicit pin → S6 prefer_pro overlay).

outcome.description: Stage outcome. `hit` = stage produced a resolution. `miss` = stage did not match; resolver advanced. `applied` = post-resolution overlay (S6 prefer_pro). `skipped` = stage gated off by config (e.g., S6 with prefer_pro_models=false). `error` = stage produced a typed failure. Per PRD FR-5.7.

label.description: Stable string identifier for the stage. Operators grep for these in FR-5.7 debug output.

details.description: Stage-specific context. Closed schema (additionalProperties:false) per cypherpunk HIGH-3 to prevent attacker-controlled extra fields leaking through to downstream consumers.

code.description: Stable error code. `[TIER-NO-MAPPING]` (FR-3.8 fail-closed at S3). `[OVERRIDE-UNKNOWN-MODEL]` (validation pre-resolution per IMP-004). `[MODEL-EXTRA-OVERRIDE-CONFLICT]` (validation pre-resolution per IMP-004). `[NO-RESOLUTION]` (resolver exhausted all 6 stages without a hit). `[INPUT-CONTROL-BYTE]` (cypherpunk HIGH-2 — input contained C0 control byte in scalar). `[YAML-PARSE-FAILED]` / `[NO-EXPECTED-RESOLUTIONS]` (fixture-level errors emitted by golden runners; only valid in the fixture-level error variant of the top-level oneOf).

stage_failed.description: Which stage produced the error (0 = pre-resolution validation; 1-6 = the FR-3.9 stages). Operators correlate with FR-5.7 trace logs.

detail.description: Human-readable error context. Operator-actionable (mentions the field, the conflict, the remediation when knowable).
